text: Alkene Hydrofunctionalization with Mes-Acr+ Mediated Photoredox Catalysis: Mechanistic Insights and Kinetic Investigations In the presence of hydrogen, alkene hydrofunctionalization with 9-mesityl-10-methylacridinium (Mes-Acr+ as a photoredox catalyst has been fully explored and an evidence for alkene cation radical (AR) intermediates has been observed. After studying various components, we demonstrated that the phenylthiyl radical (PhS) oxidized the persistent acridinyl radical (Ar), providing a direct link between photoredox catalysis and HAT. Furthermore, we studied the functional role of (PhS)2 and thiophenol, and found that they proceed through a similar catalytic pathway, which commences by breaking the disulfide bond through photolysis. Using DFT calculations, we compared the relative reactivities of the hydrogen atom donors (PhSH and 2-phenylmalononitrile (PMN). The results indicated that PhSH is favorable to act as a HAT agent over PMN and the lower activation energy of PhSH in relation to PMN is largely due to a lesser steric hindrance during the TS formation. We have also demonstrated that during the initial part of the catalysis, Mes-Acr+ was observed to give adducts off the main cycle which we speculated are due to PhS−. Eventually, we attributed the difference in reactivity toward hydrogen between (PhS)2 and PhSH toward hydrogen to the proton transfer step being rate limiting in the HAT cycle.
